message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="pink" summary="Confirming alignment, flagging sidebar question is still open">
Great, we're fully aligned on the CSS architecture patterns. Just want to make sure you saw my earlier message about Heydon's sidebar pushback -- it may have crossed paths with this one.

The short version: Heydon recommends no sidebar, all GTD links in the header nav instead (one `<nav>` landmark, simpler for screen readers). I presented you three options (A: wrapping header nav, B: header with "More" dropdown, C: single nav repositioned via CSS to bottom on mobile).

Your CSS direction for components, tokens, modifiers, and architecture all hold regardless of the layout decision. The only things affected are:
- Whether we need sidebar-specific tokens and the two-panel grid
- Whether the GTD content area is 48rem (with sidebar) or stays closer to 40-44rem (without)

I've asked Steve Krug for a usability take and flagged it to Marty for product perspective. This is the one open question before we're ready to execute.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Lea, yes -- I saw that message and already responded to it. I chose Option A (wrapping header nav) and dropped the sidebar entirely. I also sent Heydon a direct message thanking him for the pushback and asking three follow-up a11y questions.

The sidebar is dead. We're aligned on:
- Single `<nav>` landmark in the header
- Flexbox wrap for mobile (two rows is fine)
- `aria-current="page"` for active state
- No sidebar tokens, no z-index scale, no two-panel grid
- Content area at ~44rem instead of 48rem

This is settled from my end. The only open item is Heydon's response to my three a11y questions (inline count badge pattern, two-tier nav visual weight, and processing flow announcements).
input.summary: Sidebar already dropped, confirmed Option A alignment

routing.summary: Sidebar already dropped, confirmed Option A alignment
